interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working in mature SaaS
codingTopics: Streams at high throughput: engagement-event processing, Kafka-consumer idempotency
Queues and scheduling: campaign sends across rate-limited channels, greedy + heap
Sliding-window rate limiting and frequency caps
Hash-map segmentation and MongoDB-style document merging
Debugging exercises in realtime pipelines; Ruby on Rails stack
systemDesignTopics: Design the campaign-send pipeline that delivers billions of push notifications and emails daily with per-user frequency caps
Design a real-time segmentation engine that re-evaluates audience membership as user events stream in
Design the Currents export system streaming engagement events to customer data warehouses with at-least-once delivery

coding: Implement a per-user frequency-cap rate limiter (max N messages per rolling window) that holds up at a million sends a second.
From a user-event stream, evaluate segment membership rules like 'did X at least k times in the last 7 days' incrementally (sliding window).
Merge partial user-profile documents arriving out of order into one canonical document with per-field last-write-wins.
Schedule a campaign's sends across ESP connections with different throughput limits to finish soonest (greedy + heap).
Debug: a Kafka consumer double-processes engagement events after rebalances — find the bug and make processing idempotent.
behavioral: Take your seat at the table: tell me about asserting a position in a forum where you were junior.
Don't ignore smoke: describe noticing a small anomaly that turned out to be a real problem. What made you dig?
Embrace curiosity: what system did you learn end-to-end just because you wanted to understand it?
Tell me about operating a high-throughput realtime system during a traffic spike (think Black Friday sends).
systemDesign: Design the multichannel send pipeline: push, email, SMS, in-app — with per-channel throttles and global frequency caps.
Design streaming segmentation over MongoDB-backed user profiles at billions of events per day.
Design Currents: reliable event export to Kafka/warehouses with customer-visible delivery SLAs.
values: Braze's values include 'be a human in the pursuit of excellence' — how do you keep empathy while holding a high bar?
Our customers' brands live or die on message relevance. How do you think about the line between engagement and spam?
Which Braze value would you find hardest to live, and why?
other: How does Braze compare to Customer.io or Iterable?

rawSummary:
Braze Interview Guide (2026): Customer Engagement Platform
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Braze
braze.com ↗

Braze is the leading customer engagement platform — focuses on mobile marketing, push notifications, in-app messages. Public since 2021. The interview emphasizes high-throughput message delivery, ML-driven personalization, and the unique scale challenges of consumer messaging.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a push notification platform handling billions of sends per day

- Design segmentation and audience targeting at scale

- Design A/B testing for marketing campaigns

- Coding: medium DSA, often with stream processing framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, working in mature SaaS

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $155K–$195K total

- Senior SE: $225K–$295K

- Staff: $325K–$430K

- Principal: $460K–$610K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java or Scala (bulk of the codebase)

- Understand stream processing (Kafka, Flink) and high-throughput pipelines

- Brush up on push notification protocols (APNs, FCM)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Braze remote-friendly?

Hybrid in NYC (HQ), London, Tokyo. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Braze compare to Customer.io or Iterable?

Braze is largest by enterprise customers. Iterable is comparable. Customer.io is more developer-friendly. Braze pays comparable to mid-tier SaaS.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, customer-driven, mid-stage. Strong work-life balance.
